step_by_step_reasoning: We first identify the user/image input node on the whiteboard, mapping it to UserConsumerWebMobile since users upload images via SDK, API, and web/mobile apps. Following the physical whiteboard diagram, we map API Gateway, ALB, ECS, SQS, and the EC2 processing layer (running CPU/GPU workloads) as the ingestion and processing flow. For the ML pipeline, we map the 'TP' training input source, S3, SageMaker, and Lambda, which coordinates model training and syncs weights back to the EC2 instances.

graph.name: Spine.ai - High-Scale AI Cataloging Architecture
graph.notes: Handles processing of 100k images/day for digital cataloging using hybrid CPU/GPU instances and cost-effective SageMaker training.

nodes.name: IMAGE
nodes.notes: Images uploaded via SDK, API, or web/mobile.

nodes.name: ALB
nodes.notes: Routes traffic to the correct ECS cluster.

nodes.name: ECS
nodes.notes: Saves metadata to RDS and routes to processing queues.

nodes.name: Processing Layer
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: CPU instances for pre/post processing, G5 GPU instances for inference.

nodes.name: S3
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Stores training data and model weights.

nodes.name: SageMaker
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Trains models using SageMaker Spot instances.

nodes.name: Lambda
nodes.notes: Triggers weight sync to processing layer.

edges.notes: Pushes tasks to appropriate SQS queue
Syncs model weights to processing layer
